concerns.text: Task T017b requires comparing a sample against a 'simulated oracle' to calculate accuracy. This violates FR-015 ('System MUST perform a manual audit') and Constitution Principle VI (Provenance) by replacing the required human-in-the-loop verification with an artificial, non-reproducible ground truth. The task silently relaxes the 'manual' constraint of the spec.

Task T024 restricts the implementation to 'Benjamini-Hochberg' only. FR-005 explicitly allows 'Bonferroni or Benjamini-Hochberg'. By hardcoding a single choice without a design decision record or spec amendment, this task narrows the implementation scope and violates the flexibility mandated by the functional requirement.

Task T018 mandates writing an 'error_report.json' to 'data/reports/'. The spec (Assumptions/Plan) defines 'data/' strictly for raw/processed datasets and 'docs/' for reports. This task introduces a silent directory structure violation, potentially breaking the 'Data Hygiene' principle (Constitution Principle III) which requires derivations to be in 'data/' but reports in 'docs/'.

Task T013 specifies downloading from 'HuggingFace datasets.load_dataset()' but does not cite the specific dataset ID or URL. FR-001 and Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) require external citations to be verified and specific. A task that omits the specific dataset identifier risks fetching an unverified or incorrect dataset, violating the 'Verified Accuracy' constraint.
